description: Introduce a projection-first workflow state machine contract and TUI so users can identify the active path, recent changes, and active agents without transcript-based reconstruction.

userStories.title: Define workflow-progress.v2 contract
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want a versioned workflow-progress.v2 schema so that built-in and external workflows emit one deterministic state-machine payload.
acceptanceCriteria: Add workflow-progress.v2 versioned types for workflow metadata, machine metadata, step definitions, step runs, transition records, and participant metadata
Step definitions support task, group, wait, decision, and terminal kinds
Step runs support pending, active, waiting, blocked, retrying, completed, and failed statuses
Step runs and transitions carry stable ids plus deterministic ordering fields used as reducer tie-breakers
Transition records support timestamp, from-state, to-state, reason, and source references when available
workflow-progress.v1 remains accepted at the ingestion boundary during migration
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed by implement workflow after 3 review round(s). Commit: b1b90d602. US-001 is already repaired; no extra code changes were needed this round. The only listed failure was that verification had not been run, so I re-ran the smallest relevant checks for `packages/workflow-api` and they pass. - `packages/workflow-api/src/index.ts:309` exports the versioned v2 metadata aliases for workflow, machine, participant, and transition records. - `packages/workflow-api/src/inde

userStories.title: Normalize v1, v2, and partial payloads
userStories.description: As a TUI developer, I want one normalizer boundary so that fallback and version compatibility are owned in one place before projection.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a normalizer that accepts workflow-progress.v1, workflow-progress.v2, and partial payloads and returns one reducer input shape without transcript parsing
The normalizer owns fallback values for missing workflow label, step label, agent label, timestamp, round, and reason fields
Fallback output is non-empty and reused by all projection regions instead of panel-specific fallback logic
Fixtures cover v1 payloads, v2 payloads, partial metadata, group steps, repeated retries, and mixed payload variants
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed by implement workflow after 7 review round(s). Commit: a4ab2259c. Accepted unresolved review issues after clean test-runner: Architect: `packages/workflow-api/src/index.ts:1857` still returns `base` from `coerceWorkflowProgress()` when `strictv2(base)` fails, so `normalizeWorkflowProgressInput()` can accept graph-consistent but non-strict partial v2 payloads (for example with duplicate or non-monotonic transition ids/seq that `partial()` does not reject). Downstream code in `packages/opencode/src/workflow/index.ts:879` . Fixed the US-002 reviewer regressions by making the normalizer boundary actually canonical and restoring `workflowproject()` compatibility for raw callers. - `packages/workflow-api/src/index.ts:1798` now makes `normalizeWorkflowProgressInput()` always return one `WorkflowProgressRead` shape, so downstream code no longer has to understand strict-vs-read internals or call `coerceWorkflowProgress()`

userStories.title: Build deterministic workflow projection reducer
userStories.description: As a TUI developer, I want one projection reducer so that the workflow screen renders stable regions from state-machine data instead of inferred text.
acceptanceCriteria: Reducer output includes header, timeline, agents, history, and alerts regions from normalized input
Active-path selection is deterministic when parallel branches are active, branch metadata is incomplete, or group children have different statuses
Latest meaningful transition selection ignores duplicate or no-op records and breaks ties with deterministic ordering fields instead of array order
Agents projection derives current state and latest action from participant metadata plus step-run and transition source references without transcript parsing
Alerts projection emits distinct running, waiting, blocked, retrying, failed, and done states for both rich and fallback payloads
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed by implement workflow after 7 review round(s). Commit: 9a733cc24c81d6726f65a811ba35332731eb2806. Accepted unresolved review issues after clean test-runner: Architect: `packages/workflow-api/src/presentation.ts:483` and `packages/workflow-api/src/presentation.ts:496` choose a live descendant for a group by step ancestry only, not by the active group run instance. If a group step is retried/re-entered and an older child run is still live or partially stale, `current()` can project the wrong branch, which then cascades into wrong timeline focus and step. Built the deterministic workflow projection reducer in `packages/workflow-api/src/presentation.ts:44` and tightened the selection logic across all projected regions. - `packages/workflow-api/src/presentation.ts:445` adds a canonical `changes()` pass that filters no-op transitions, collapses duplicates, and sorts with deterministic tie-breakers instead of relying on input order. - `packages/workflo

userStories.title: Wire the live workflow screen to projection state
userStories.description: As a user, I want the real workflow screen to read projection state directly so that live updates do not depend on transcript reconstruction.
acceptanceCriteria: Live workflow updates feed the normalizer and reducer pipeline before render, and the workflow screen reads projection state instead of parsing transcript text on paint
The workflow screen container accepts both fixture data and runtime updates through the same projection boundary
The MVP workflow screen adds no pause, resume, cancel, retry, or manual-step controls or keybindings
A zero-data workflow screen state renders without crashing and does not fall back to transcript parsing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed by implement workflow after 7 review round(s). Commit: 77a2ac81293dbcfebda4ed0c57736d97f483941b. Accepted unresolved review issues after clean test-runner: QA: [warning] `packages/opencode/test/cli/workflow-tool-reactive.test.ts:44` places `15000` inside the spawned `bun -e` script instead of on the outer `Bun.spawnSync(..., { timeout })` call, so this test has no process-level timeout. If the OpenTUI render deadlocks or the child process hangs, CI can stall instead of failing fast. userStories.title: Emit a thin implement workflow v2 slice
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one real workflow to emit the new contract early so that runtime assumptions are validated before panel work finishes.
acceptanceCriteria: .opencode/workflows/implement.ts emits workflow-progress.v2 updates for a linear path that includes convert, story selection, implement or fix, review gate, and done or failed
Runtime emission produces explicit step definitions, step runs, and transitions consumed by the live projection pipeline end to end
An integration fixture or test proves emitted v2 data reaches the workflow screen without workflow-specific rendering logic
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed by implement workflow after 11 review round(s). Commit: a1864f7d37e839045a0b3552561e7cd9feef1900.

userStories.title: Build the workflow shell and verification harness
userStories.description: As a TUI developer, I want a stable workflow shell and harness so that panel work is verifiable at fixed terminal widths and degraded states.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a workflow screen shell with stable region order: header, timeline, agents, history, alerts
The shell has deterministic layouts for 120-column and 80-column widths, and 80-column output keeps region order while stacking or truncating within panel bounds
Add a fixture-driven workflow screen harness that can load projection states for browser and snapshot verification
Harness fixtures cover running, waiting, blocked, retrying, failed, done, workflow-progress.v1 fallback, and partial-metadata states at 120 and 80 columns
The shell includes explicit empty and no-active-workflow states using shared normalizer fallback tokens
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render the workflow header panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a stable workflow header so that I can identify the run, current phase, and overall status immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Header reads only the header projection region
Header shows workflow name, goal or input summary, overall status, current phase, and start time when available
When any header field is missing, the panel renders the shared normalizer fallback token instead of blank text or panel-specific copy
Header visibly distinguishes running, waiting, blocked, failed, and done states
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render the active-path timeline panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a timeline focused on the chosen execution path so that I can see where the workflow is without scanning the full graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline reads only the timeline projection region
Timeline highlights the active step run and keeps completed ancestors on the chosen path visible
The MVP timeline omits inactive pending branches and renders only the active branch plus active group children
Group, wait, decision, and terminal step kinds render distinct labels or tokens from projection data
Snapshots cover grouped work, waiting state, blocked state, retrying state, failed state, and terminal or done state at 120 and 80 columns
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Render the agents panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which agents or roles are active so that I can tell who is working, waiting, blocked, retrying, or done.
acceptanceCriteria: Agents panel reads only the agents projection region
Each row shows participant or role label, current state, and latest action summary derived by the reducer
Missing participant names or summaries fall back to the shared normalizer tokens instead of panel-specific copy
Visual states differ for active, waiting, blocked, retrying, and done
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Render the history panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want recent transitions and round summaries so that I can understand what changed most recently without reading raw logs.
acceptanceCriteria: History panel reads only the history projection region
Recent transitions render in deterministic order using reducer ordering fields, with the newest meaningful change visible in the first row
Round metadata renders as compact round summary rows when present, and missing round metadata does not break the panel layout
Snapshots cover repeated retries, duplicate timestamps, and mixed step-level and workflow-level transitions
Tests pass

userStories.title: Document external workflow adoption and fallback
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want validated migration guidance so that built-in and external workflows can adopt the shared contract without custom TUI logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation states that workflow-progress.v2 is the public contract for built-in and external workflows, and any helper emitter API is optional convenience only
Documentation explains safe degradation for workflow-progress.v1, workflow-progress.v2, and partial metadata across header, timeline, agents, history, and alerts
Add one executable external-style example that emits step definitions, step runs, and transitions and renders through the shared normalizer, reducer, and TUI path without workflow-specific UI code
Documentation calls out the MVP read-only constraint and absence of operator controls
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
